title: 2026-05-28 Active English Memorization Deck

sentences: I need to make time for English every day.
I should make time for studying English after work.
I want to make time for things that really matter.
transform: I need to make time for reading every night.
I should make time for speaking practice every day.
items.prompt: Use make time for to describe one real plan for tonight.

items.meaning: 通过做某事让任务更容易
sentences: I can make it easier by starting small.
I can make English learning easier by writing three simple sentences every day.
I can make it easier by putting it into my daily plan.
transform: I can make reading easier by reading only one page first.
I can make speaking easier by recording one short answer.
items.prompt: Say one thing you can do to make English easier.

sentences: It’s not that I don’t have time. I just haven’t made a clear plan.
It’s not that I don’t want to learn English. I just don’t use it enough.
It’s not that the task is too hard. I just need to start with a smaller step.
transform: It’s not that I don’t like reading. I just haven’t made it part of my daily routine.
It’s not that I can’t speak English. I just need more practice.
items.prompt: Use It’s not that..., I just... to explain your English-learning problem.

items.pattern: keep doing / keep a regular habit
items.meaning: 持续做某事 / 保持规律习惯
sentences: I want to keep studying every day.
I need to keep a regular study habit.
If I keep doing a little every day, I will improve.
transform: I want to keep reading simple English stories.
I need to keep practicing useful chunks.
items.prompt: Use keep doing to describe a habit you want to build.

items.pattern: remember it for a short time
items.meaning: 短时间记住，而不是“short memory”
sentences: I may remember it for a short time, but I will forget it later.
If I only study once, I can only remember it for a short time.
A small daily system helps me remember things for longer.
transform: If I only read a word once, I may remember it for a short time.
If I use it in my own sentence, I can remember it for longer.
items.prompt: Explain why using a sentence helps you remember a word for longer.

items.meaning: will 后面直接接动词原形，不加 to
sentences: I will read one short English story tonight.
I will watch one American video and listen to how people speak.
I will write three sentences before I go to bed.
transform: I will practice speaking for five minutes.
I will memorize useful chunks, not only single words.
items.prompt: Write three future-plan sentences without using will to.

items.pattern: memorize words / memory is a noun
items.meaning: memorize 是动词，memory 是名词
sentences: I don’t want to only memorize words.
I want to memorize useful chunks and use them in real sentences.
Good memory helps, but active use matters more.
transform: I memorize better when I use the word in my own sentence.
I don’t just memorize; I practice using the words.
items.prompt: Explain why memorizing words is not enough.

items.meaning: every day = 每天；everyday = 日常的
sentences: I study English every day.
I want to make English part of my everyday life.
A small everyday habit can bring a big change over time.
transform: I use simple English in everyday situations.
items.prompt: Make two sentences: one with every day and one with everyday.

recap_questions: Which sentence feels most useful today?
Can you say it without looking?
Can you change it to your own real situation?
